title: Job Openings at Rubels & Angels
content:
Rubels & Angels is a Premium Dining and Take out Restaurant redefining the foodservice industry.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Pastry Chef / Cook

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	The Pastry Chef/Cook is responsible for preparing, cooking, baking, and presenting a wide range of meals, pastries, desserts, and baked products in accordance with the company’s quality standards.
	The role requires creativity, attention to detail, excellent culinary skills, and strict adherence to food safety, hygiene, and sanitation regulations.
	The successful candidate will ensure efficient kitchen operations, proper inventory management, and consistent delivery of high-quality food and bakery products.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare and cook a variety of local and continental dishes.
	Prepare, bake, and decorate cakes, pastries, breads, cookies, desserts, and other baked products.
	Ensure all meals and baked items meet established quality, taste, and presentation standards.
	Develop and introduce new recipes and menu items where required.
	Follow standardized recipes, portion control, and production schedules.
	Ensure timely preparation and delivery of customer orders.
	Maintain cleanliness and sanitation of the kitchen, bakery, equipment, and workstations.
	Ensure proper handling, storage, and preservation of food and baking ingredients.
	Monitor stock levels and prepare inventory and requisition reports.
	Minimize food wastage and ensure cost-effective use of ingredients.
	Inspect ingredients to ensure freshness and quality.
	Operate kitchen and bakery equipment safely and efficiently.
	Comply with all food safety, health, and hygiene regulations.
	Supervise or assist junior kitchen staff where necessary.
	Report equipment faults and maintenance needs promptly.
	Perform any other duties assigned by Management.


Requirements
Educational Qualification:


	Minimum of SSCE / WAEC / NECO.
	Diploma or Certificate in Culinary Arts, Baking and Pastry, Hospitality Management, or a related field is an added advantage.
	Relevant food safety or hygiene certification is desirable.


Experience:


	 2–5 years of relevant experience as a Cook, Pastry Chef, or in a similar role.
	Experience in restaurants, hotels, bakeries, cafés, or hospitality establishments is preferred.
	Proven ability to prepare both meals and baked products to a high standard.


Other Requirements:


	High level of integrity, professionalism, and reliability.
	Physically fit and able to stand for extended periods.
	Willingness to work flexible hours, including weekends, public holidays, and shifts.
	Strong commitment to cleanliness, quality, and customer satisfaction.
	Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.
	Positive attitude and willingness to learn new culinary techniques.
	Ability to thrive in a fast-paced kitchen environment.


Required Skills:


	Excellent cooking and baking skills.
	Strong knowledge of pastry production and food preparation techniques.
	Creativity in food presentation and menu development.
	Good understanding of food safety and hygiene practices.
	Strong organizational and time management skills.
	Ability to work efficiently under pressure.
	Excellent attention to detail.
	Good communication and teamwork skills.
	Inventory and stock management skills.
	Ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment.


Remuneration


	Salary: N70,000 - N200,000 per month.
	Competitive salary based on qualifications and experience.
	Performance-based incentives (where applicable).
	Staff meals during working hours.
	Opportunities for professional development and career growth.
	Other benefits in line with the Company’s Human Resources policy.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVand cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

2.) Mixologist

Job Location: Akoka (Yoba), Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	This for the responsibilities of hygienically preparing cocktail, mocktail, alcoholic and non alcoholic drinks for customers satisfaction. Skills include serum making and good drink presentation.


Requirements


	OND,HND,BSC and additional qualification, Prior experience is a plus.


Salary
N120,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.) Store Keeper

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Branch Manager / Operations Manager

Job Summary


	The Store Keeper is responsible for receiving, storing, issuing, and maintaining accurate records of all inventory items within the branch.
	The role ensures that stock levels are properly monitored, losses are minimized, and all materials required for daily operations are available when needed.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and record incoming goods and supplies.
	Maintain accurate inventory and stock records.
	Issue materials and supplies to authorized personnel.
	Monitor stock levels and initiate replenishment requests when necessary.
	Conduct periodic stock counts and inventory audits.
	Ensure proper storage and organization of inventory items.
	Track stock movement and report discrepancies promptly.
	Maintain cleanliness, safety, and orderliness within the store.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly inventory reports.
	Prevent wastage, theft, and misuse of company property.
	Collaborate with management during stock verification exercises.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Inventory accuracy.
	Stock loss reduction.
	Timely stock replenishment.
	Proper documentation and reporting.
	Compliance with inventory control procedures.
	Store cleanliness and organization.


Requirements
Required Skills:


	Inventory management skills.
	Record-keeping and documentation skills.
	Basic computer proficiency (Microsoft Excel and Word).
	Attention to detail and accuracy.
	Organizational and time management skills.
	Numerical and analytical skills.
	Integrity and accountability.
	Effective communication skills.
	Ability to work under minimal supervision.


Educational Qualification:


	Minimum of OND, HND, or Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Logistics, Supply Chain Management, or related fields is an added advantage.


Experience:


	Minimum of 1 year of experience in inventory management, storekeeping, warehousing, or a related role.
	Experience within the hospitality, retail, restaurant, or FMCG industry is an added advantage.


Other Requirements:


	Must possess a high level of honesty and integrity.
	Must be physically fit and capable of handling inventory-related tasks.
	Must be able to maintain confidentiality of company records.
	Ability to work weekends, public holidays, and shifts when required.
	Must be resident within a reasonable commuting distance from the assigned branch.
	Must be willing to comply with all company policies and procedures.


Remuneration


	Salary: Competitive and based on qualification and experience.
	Performance incentives where applicable.
	Opportunities for training and career development.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their cover letter containg your qualification, phone number, email address and other essential info to get in touch with you and CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Officer
Job Location: Lekki Free Zone, Ibeju Lekki - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Functional Area: Supply Chain

Responsibilities


	Base operations coordination.
	Inbound and outbound goods validations and revalidations.
	Timely dispatch of validated goods.
	Form M and PAAR processing.
	Adherence to supply chain processes and procedures
	Weekly logistics reporting.
	iaising with freight forwarders and clearing agents.
	Coordinating port operations in collaboration with clearing agents.
	Planning &amp; Coordinating skills, including shipment scheduling, resource allocation, route optimization, and contingency planning.
	Understanding of the entire logistics process, from sourcing to warehousing and delivery.
	Risk and compliance management: Knowledge of import/export legislation and safety compliance issues.
	Oilfield materials management, including OCTG handling and storage.
	Coordinating transportation, delivery and ensuring regulatory compliance.


Person Specification:


	University Degree
	Minimum of 3 years of experience
	Ideal candidate: Honest and hardworking, confident with good social skills, team player, resourceful, energetic, agile learner, detail-oriented.
	Must be able to drive and have a valid driver&rsquo;s license.


Required Competencies:


	Knowledge of transportation management is necessary for ensuring efficient movement of goods.
	Understanding and adhering to regulations related to global trade is required for international operations.
	Problem Solving skills, ability to quickly identify, analyse and resolve issues that arise in logistics operations.
	Effective Communication skills
	Excellent interpersonal &amp; relationship management skills, attention to detail.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Note:


	Preference will be given to candidates who currently live around the Ajah/ElekoAxis of Lagos or other areas not too far from the Lekki Free Zone, Ibeju Lekki, Lagos.
	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Logistics Officer Job at Bell OIl and Gas Limited




        
    

        
        Bell OIl and Gas Limited is a leadingindigenous oilfield services company, dedicated to consistently proffering solutions, driving local content development and capacity building in Nigeria. We offer an extensive range of high quality professional services to clients across the facets of oil and gas industry and other industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Officer
Job Location: Lekki Free Zone, Ibeju Lekki - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Functional Area: Supply Chain

Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Base operations coordination.
	Inbound and outbound goods validations and revalidations.
	Timely dispatch of validated goods.
	Form M and PAAR processing.
	Adherence to supply chain processes and procedures
	Weekly logistics reporting.
	iaising with freight forwarders and clearing agents.
	Coordinating port operations in collaboration with clearing agents.
	Planning & Coordinating skills, including shipment scheduling, resource allocation, route optimization, and contingency planning.
	Understanding of the entire logistics process, from sourcing to warehousing and delivery.
	Risk and compliance management: Knowledge of import/export legislation and safety compliance issues.
	Oilfield materials management, including OCTG handling and storage.
	Coordinating transportation, delivery and ensuring regulatory compliance.


Person Specification:


	University Degree
	Minimum of 3 years of experience
	Ideal candidate: Honest and hardworking, confident with good social skills, team player, resourceful, energetic, agile learner, detail-oriented.
	Must be able to drive and have a valid driver’s license.


Required Competencies:


	Knowledge of transportation management is necessary for ensuring efficient movement of goods.
	Understanding and adhering to regulations related to global trade is required for international operations.
	Problem Solving skills, ability to quickly identify, analyse and resolve issues that arise in logistics operations.
	Effective Communication skills
	Excellent interpersonal & relationship management skills, attention to detail.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send an updated CV as an attachment  to: [email protected] using the Job Position  as the subject of the email.

Note:


	Preference will be given to candidates who currently live around the Ajah/ElekoAxis of Lagos or other areas not too far from the Lekki Free Zone, Ibeju Lekki, Lagos.
	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Office Manager",
  "description": "Luxsit Integration Limited is a smart home solution company located in the heart of Lagos.Our main focus is to bring people and technology together. We handle all aspects of your technology project including design, scheduling, project management, installation, and service and support. We are a customer service-oriented company and strive for complete customer satisfaction. With our experienced and trained dedicated team of tech experts, bringing your dreams to reality. At Luxsit integration &ndash; whether the project is new construction or retrofit, we integrate seamlessly with all involved to deliver custom, lifestyle inspired solutions across the spectrum of automation, audio/video, cinema rooms, lighting, climate, and security.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Office Manager
Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Supervise administrative staff, including receptionists, administrative assistants, and clerical personnel, providing guidance, training, and support to ensure efficient and effective performance.
	Plan and coordinate office operations and procedures, establishing workflows, systems, and protocols to optimize efficiency, productivity, and organization within the office.
	Manage office facilities and equipment, overseeing maintenance, repairs, and procurement of supplies, furniture, and technology to support daily operations and employee needs.
	Coordinate office logistics, including scheduling meetings, appointments, and events, arranging travel and accommodations, and managing calendars and itineraries for executives and staff.
	Oversee office communication channels, including phone systems, email accounts, and internal messaging platforms, ensuring timely and effective communication among staff and stakeholders.
	Handle administrative tasks, such as drafting correspondence, preparing reports, processing invoices, managing expense reports, and maintaining records and files in compliance with organizational policies and procedures.
	Serve as a point of contact for internal and external stakeholders, including clients, vendors, and visitors, addressing inquiries, resolving issues, and providing assistance as needed.
	Implement and enforce office policies, procedures, and standards, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements, health and safety guidelines, and company protocols.
	Manage office budgets and expenses, tracking expenditures, reconciling accounts, and preparing financial reports and forecasts to support financial planning and decision-making.
	Coordinate office security measures, including access control, surveillance systems, and emergency preparedness plans, to safeguard personnel, assets, and facilities.
	Foster a positive and inclusive office culture, promoting teamwork, collaboration, and employee engagement through team-building activities, recognition programs, and professional development opportunities.
	Conduct performance evaluations and provide feedback to administrative staff, recognizing achievements and addressing areas for improvement to enhance individual and team effectiveness.
	Collaborate with other departments and stakeholders to support cross-functional projects, initiatives, and special events, contributing administrative expertise and resources as needed.
	Stay updated on industry trends, best practices, and technological advancements in office management and administration, continuously seeking opportunities for process improvement and innovation.
	Represent the organization in a professional manner, both internally and externally, demonstrating integrity, professionalism, and discretion in all interactions and communications.


Requirements and Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s degree in business administration, office management, or a related field; relevant work experience may be considered in lieu of formal education.
	Proven experience in office management, administrative leadership, or related roles, with a track record of success in managing office operations and supporting organizational objectives.
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks, manage competing priorities, and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to interact professionally with staff, clients, vendors, and other stakeholders at all levels of the organization.
	Proficiency in office software applications, including Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) and cloud-based collaboration tools, such as Google Workspace or Microsoft 365.
	Attention to detail and accuracy in data entry, document preparation, and recordkeeping, ensuring completeness, correctness, and confidentiality of information.
	Leadership and supervisory abilities, with the capacity to motivate, mentor, and develop administrative staff to achieve individual and team goals and performance targets.
	Problem-solving and decision-making skills, with the ability to analyze situations, identify issues, and implement effective solutions to resolve problems and improve processes.
	Adaptability and flexibility in responding to changing needs, priorities, and circumstances, with a proactive and solutions-oriented approach to managing office operations.
	Commitment to professionalism, integrity, and confidentiality, maintaining discretion and confidentiality in handling sensitive information and personnel matters.


Required Skills:


	Office management
	Administrative skills
	Communication skills
	Organizational skills
	Leadership
	Problem-solving
	Multitasking
	Attention to detail
	Technology proficiency
	Adaptability.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: lillianowoseni@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

}



Office Manager Job at Luxsit Integration Limited




        
    

        
        Luxsit Integration Limited is a smart home solution company located in the heart of Lagos.Our main focus is to bring people and technology together. We handle all aspects of your technology project including design, scheduling, project management, installation, and service and support. We are a customer service-oriented company and strive for complete customer satisfaction. With our experienced and trained dedicated team of tech experts, bringing your dreams to reality. At Luxsit integration – whether the project is new construction or retrofit, we integrate seamlessly with all involved to deliver custom, lifestyle inspired solutions across the spectrum of automation, audio/video, cinema rooms, lighting, climate, and security.
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Office Manager
Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Supervise administrative staff, including receptionists, administrative assistants, and clerical personnel, providing guidance, training, and support to ensure efficient and effective performance.
	Plan and coordinate office operations and procedures, establishing workflows, systems, and protocols to optimize efficiency, productivity, and organization within the office.
	Manage office facilities and equipment, overseeing maintenance, repairs, and procurement of supplies, furniture, and technology to support daily operations and employee needs.
	Coordinate office logistics, including scheduling meetings, appointments, and events, arranging travel and accommodations, and managing calendars and itineraries for executives and staff.
	Oversee office communication channels, including phone systems, email accounts, and internal messaging platforms, ensuring timely and effective communication among staff and stakeholders.
	Handle administrative tasks, such as drafting correspondence, preparing reports, processing invoices, managing expense reports, and maintaining records and files in compliance with organizational policies and procedures.
	Serve as a point of contact for internal and external stakeholders, including clients, vendors, and visitors, addressing inquiries, resolving issues, and providing assistance as needed.
	Implement and enforce office policies, procedures, and standards, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements, health and safety guidelines, and company protocols.
	Manage office budgets and expenses, tracking expenditures, reconciling accounts, and preparing financial reports and forecasts to support financial planning and decision-making.
	Coordinate office security measures, including access control, surveillance systems, and emergency preparedness plans, to safeguard personnel, assets, and facilities.
	Foster a positive and inclusive office culture, promoting teamwork, collaboration, and employee engagement through team-building activities, recognition programs, and professional development opportunities.
	Conduct performance evaluations and provide feedback to administrative staff, recognizing achievements and addressing areas for improvement to enhance individual and team effectiveness.
	Collaborate with other departments and stakeholders to support cross-functional projects, initiatives, and special events, contributing administrative expertise and resources as needed.
	Stay updated on industry trends, best practices, and technological advancements in office management and administration, continuously seeking opportunities for process improvement and innovation.
	Represent the organization in a professional manner, both internally and externally, demonstrating integrity, professionalism, and discretion in all interactions and communications.


Requirements and Qualifications


	Bachelor's degree in business administration, office management, or a related field; relevant work experience may be considered in lieu of formal education.
	Proven experience in office management, administrative leadership, or related roles, with a track record of success in managing office operations and supporting organizational objectives.
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks, manage competing priorities, and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to interact professionally with staff, clients, vendors, and other stakeholders at all levels of the organization.
	Proficiency in office software applications, including Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) and cloud-based collaboration tools, such as Google Workspace or Microsoft 365.
	Attention to detail and accuracy in data entry, document preparation, and recordkeeping, ensuring completeness, correctness, and confidentiality of information.
	Leadership and supervisory abilities, with the capacity to motivate, mentor, and develop administrative staff to achieve individual and team goals and performance targets.
	Problem-solving and decision-making skills, with the ability to analyze situations, identify issues, and implement effective solutions to resolve problems and improve processes.
	Adaptability and flexibility in responding to changing needs, priorities, and circumstances, with a proactive and solutions-oriented approach to managing office operations.
	Commitment to professionalism, integrity, and confidentiality, maintaining discretion and confidentiality in handling sensitive information and personnel matters.


Required Skills:


	Office management
	Administrative skills
	Communication skills
	Organizational skills
	Leadership
	Problem-solving
	Multitasking
	Attention to detail
	Technology proficiency
	Adaptability.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Financial Reporting Accountant",
  "description": "WTS Energy - Our client is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Financial Reporting Accountant

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time (Hybrid)

Job Description


	Our client is seeking a Financial Reporting Accountant to lead comprehensive financial reporting and control functions within its finance department.
	As a key player in the energy sector, this role involves overseeing the preparation of financial statements, ensuring compliance with IFRS and local GAAP, and managing multi-entity financial consolidation.
	The position requires driving budgeting and forecasting processes, optimizing tax planning and regulatory compliance, and strengthening internal controls to safeguard company assets. Candidates will lead audit processes, provide strategic financial insights, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to enhance financial discipline and governance.
	This opportunity offers a dynamic environment to influence financial strategy, develop a high-performance finance team, and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives across the organization.


Responsibilities


	Oversee the preparation and accuracy of monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements in compliance with IFRS, local GAAP, and internal policies
	Maintain and enhance robust internal control systems to safeguard company assets and ensure financial integrity
	Lead the external audit process, managing relationships with auditors and driving continuous improvements in financial reporting processes and systems
	Conduct comprehensive financial and economic analyses, including investment appraisals using NPV and IRR methodologies, to support strategic decision-making
	Lead the annual budgeting process and periodic forecasting, monitoring budget performance and providing detailed variance analysis
	Collaborate with department heads to enforce financial accountability, optimize costs, and uphold financial discipline across the organization
	Oversee all corporate tax activities, ensuring timely compliance with tax regulations and developing tax-efficient strategies within legal frameworks
	Manage multi-entity financial reporting, ensuring consistency in accounting policies, consolidation of financial statements, and intercompany reconciliations
	Lead, mentor, and develop the finance team to build a high-performance function while fostering collaboration with operations, commercial, and executive leadership
	Identify financial risks and implement effective mitigation strategies, strengthening governance, compliance frameworks, and internal audit readiness to promote a culture of accountability and transparency


Requirements
Required Qualifications:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline
	Minimum of 10&ndash;12 years of progressive experience in finance, including at least 5&ndash;7 years in senior management roles
	Professional certification such as ACA, ACCA, CPA, or equivalent
	Demonstrated expertise in financial reporting and consolidation, tax planning and compliance, budgeting and forecasting, multi-entity financial management, and financial controls and governance
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting standards (IFRS/local GAAP) and industry-specific accounting practices
	Proficiency in ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle) and advanced skills in Excel and financial modeling
	Proven leadership and team management capabilities with excellent communication and presentation skills


Preferred Qualifications:


	Master&rsquo;s Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field
	Experience within the energy sector or a comparable industry
	Strategic thinking and business acumen with a track record of driving continuous improvement initiatives
	Ability to lead complex external audit processes and manage relationships with auditors and tax authorities
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience in financial and economic investment analysis (e.g., NPV, IRR)
",

Financial Reporting Accountant Job at WTS Energy




        
    

        
        WTS Energy - Our client is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Financial Reporting Accountant

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time (Hybrid)

Job Description
     

            
    




	Our client is seeking a Financial Reporting Accountant to lead comprehensive financial reporting and control functions within its finance department.
	As a key player in the energy sector, this role involves overseeing the preparation of financial statements, ensuring compliance with IFRS and local GAAP, and managing multi-entity financial consolidation.
	The position requires driving budgeting and forecasting processes, optimizing tax planning and regulatory compliance, and strengthening internal controls to safeguard company assets. Candidates will lead audit processes, provide strategic financial insights, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to enhance financial discipline and governance.
	This opportunity offers a dynamic environment to influence financial strategy, develop a high-performance finance team, and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives across the organization.


Responsibilities


	Oversee the preparation and accuracy of monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements in compliance with IFRS, local GAAP, and internal policies
	Maintain and enhance robust internal control systems to safeguard company assets and ensure financial integrity
	Lead the external audit process, managing relationships with auditors and driving continuous improvements in financial reporting processes and systems
	Conduct comprehensive financial and economic analyses, including investment appraisals using NPV and IRR methodologies, to support strategic decision-making
	Lead the annual budgeting process and periodic forecasting, monitoring budget performance and providing detailed variance analysis
	Collaborate with department heads to enforce financial accountability, optimize costs, and uphold financial discipline across the organization
	Oversee all corporate tax activities, ensuring timely compliance with tax regulations and developing tax-efficient strategies within legal frameworks
	Manage multi-entity financial reporting, ensuring consistency in accounting policies, consolidation of financial statements, and intercompany reconciliations
	Lead, mentor, and develop the finance team to build a high-performance function while fostering collaboration with operations, commercial, and executive leadership
	Identify financial risks and implement effective mitigation strategies, strengthening governance, compliance frameworks, and internal audit readiness to promote a culture of accountability and transparency


Requirements
Required Qualifications:


	Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline
	Minimum of 10–12 years of progressive experience in finance, including at least 5–7 years in senior management roles
	Professional certification such as ACA, ACCA, CPA, or equivalent
	Demonstrated expertise in financial reporting and consolidation, tax planning and compliance, budgeting and forecasting, multi-entity financial management, and financial controls and governance
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting standards (IFRS/local GAAP) and industry-specific accounting practices
	Proficiency in ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle) and advanced skills in Excel and financial modeling
	Proven leadership and team management capabilities with excellent communication and presentation skills


Preferred Qualifications:


	Master’s Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field
	Experience within the energy sector or a comparable industry
	Strategic thinking and business acumen with a track record of driving continuous improvement initiatives
	Ability to lead complex external audit processes and manage relationships with auditors and tax authorities
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience in financial and economic investment analysis (e.g., NPV, IRR)
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Electrical Engineer",
  "description": "Erkang Pharmaceuticals is a multinational manufacturer of pharmaceutical products starting up in Ilorin, Kwara State Nigeria. Erkang Pharmaceutical is one of the largest manufacturers of Large Volume Parenterals (Infusion) and other products in the world.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Engineer

Job Location: Ilorin, Kwara
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The electrical engineer in our LVP (Large Volume Parenterals) infusion company ensures the continuous, sterile, and compliant operation of IV fluid manufacturing machinery.
	They manage power distribution, industrial automation, and equipment maintenance, ensuring 100% uptime while strictly adhering to pharmaceutical safety and regulatory standards


Key Responsibilities


	Equipment Maintenance: Execute Preventative and Predictive Maintenance (PPM) on critical pharmaceutical equipment, including auto-claves, water purification (WFI) systems, and filling lines to prevent production downtime
	Automation &amp; Control: Troubleshoot and program PLCs (Programmable Logic Controllers), SCADA systems, and VFDs (Variable Frequency Drives) used to regulate fluid mixing, heating, and packaging.
	Regulatory Compliance: Ensure all machinery and facility electrical systems comply with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP), WHO, and local regulatory standards.
	Power &amp; Utility Management: Supervise the plant&rsquo;s electrical power systems, including medium/low voltage switchgears, transformers, emergency diesel generators, and uninterrupted power supplies (UPS.
	Root Cause Analysis: Diagnose electrical faults, perform root-cause analysis (RCA) on machine failures, and implement modifications to improve efficiency.
	Documentation: Maintain rigorous logs of electrical inspections, equipment calibrations, and ISO standard documentations.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Education: Bachelor&rsquo;s degree (BSc / BEng) in Electrical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, or a closely related field.
	Experience: 7 to 10 years of hands-on electrical engineering experience, preferably within Large Volume Parenterals (LVP) pharmaceutical production.
	Technical Skills: Deep understanding of industrial instrumentation, relay logic, control panels, and electrical design software (e.g., AutoCAD, ETAP)
	Soft Skills: Strong analytical problem-solving skills, emergency response capabilities, and the ability to collaborate effectively across multidisciplinary production teams


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hr.erkangpharma@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-30",
  "validThrough": "2026-08-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",

Electrical Engineer Job at Erkang Pharmaceuticals




        
    

        
        Erkang Pharmaceuticals is a multinational manufacturer of pharmaceutical products starting up in Ilorin, Kwara State Nigeria. Erkang Pharmaceutical is one of the largest manufacturers of Large Volume Parenterals (Infusion) and other products in the world.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Engineer

Job Location: Ilorin, Kwara
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	The electrical engineer in our LVP (Large Volume Parenterals) infusion company ensures the continuous, sterile, and compliant operation of IV fluid manufacturing machinery.
	They manage power distribution, industrial automation, and equipment maintenance, ensuring 100% uptime while strictly adhering to pharmaceutical safety and regulatory standards


Key Responsibilities


	Equipment Maintenance: Execute Preventative and Predictive Maintenance (PPM) on critical pharmaceutical equipment, including auto-claves, water purification (WFI) systems, and filling lines to prevent production downtime
	Automation & Control: Troubleshoot and program PLCs (Programmable Logic Controllers), SCADA systems, and VFDs (Variable Frequency Drives) used to regulate fluid mixing, heating, and packaging.
	Regulatory Compliance: Ensure all machinery and facility electrical systems comply with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP), WHO, and local regulatory standards.
	Power & Utility Management: Supervise the plant’s electrical power systems, including medium/low voltage switchgears, transformers, emergency diesel generators, and uninterrupted power supplies (UPS.
	Root Cause Analysis: Diagnose electrical faults, perform root-cause analysis (RCA) on machine failures, and implement modifications to improve efficiency.
	Documentation: Maintain rigorous logs of electrical inspections, equipment calibrations, and ISO standard documentations.


Qualifications & Requirements


	Education: Bachelor’s degree (BSc / BEng) in Electrical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, or a closely related field.
	Experience: 7 to 10 years of hands-on electrical engineering experience, preferably within Large Volume Parenterals (LVP) pharmaceutical production.
	Technical Skills: Deep understanding of industrial instrumentation, relay logic, control panels, and electrical design software (e.g., AutoCAD, ETAP)
	Soft Skills: Strong analytical problem-solving skills, emergency response capabilities, and the ability to collaborate effectively across multidisciplinary production teams


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
